VerbalDescription: Keep the same grid. Fill vertical spaces inside the rectangular objects with green colored squares and horizontal spaces with orange colored squares.
Fill hollow portion of horizontal rectangles with orange, fill hollow portion of vertical rectangles with green. Keep everything else the same.

Create a grid exactly the same size as the original. Copy the original shapes exactly as they appear in the original, the same size and colour.  Colour in these outline shapes green if they are a vertical area or orange if the area to colour in is horizontal.
Copy grid from input. Use the flood fill button to add colour to the shapes. All vertical shapes are filled with green, all horizontal lines are filled with orange.
Copy from input and then you need to fill in the vertical rectangles with the colour green, and then you fill in the horizontal rectangles with the colour orange.
Duplicate the picture and fill in vertical shapes with green, and horizontal with orange
The grid should match the input. Any vertically-oriented shape should be filled in with green, and any horizontally-oriented shape should be filled in with orange.

Keep the grid the same as the demonstration image. Colour in the boxes in the middle of the shapes with either green if the black middle boxes are going in a vertical line or orange for any horizontal lines.

Resize the grid to the height and width that reflects the grid shown. Recreate the objects in the same orientation and color as show in the first grid. In the black/empty space INSDIE of the objects, objects that are have more height than width will have the empty/black cells filled in with green cells, objects that are have more width than height will have their empty/black cells filled with orange.
Copy the input then fill in the vertical rectangles with green and the horizontal ones with orange.

Copy the original grid and its elements as is. Make a distinction between all the objects which have a greater height than width, and all the objects which are the opposite. For all objects which are more tall than wide, fill in the insides of them with the color green. For all objects which are more wide than tall, fill in the insides with orange.
Same grid size, fill centres of vertical shapes with green and horizontal shapes with orange.

On your screen there will be a number of shapes, each of which are an outline with an empty middle section. The solution will be to copy across the same shapes / grid as the input, and then to shade in the blank cells. The blank cells will either need to be shaded orange or green. You should shade them green if the shape is taller than it is wide (ie vertical) or orange if it is wider than it is tall (ie horizontal).

Same grid size and colours. The inside area of each shape needs to be filled with either green or orange. If a shape has a bigger height than width the inside should be filled with orange. If the height is smaller than the width then the inside should be filled with orange
copy from input and fill the middle of the standing boxes with green and the horizontal boxes with orange
use the same grid as in the input. There will be multiple objects in the input; in your answer, if the object has more height than width,color the inside with green else color the inside with orange

Resize the grid to match the input. Copy the shape from the input. Add a line of light blue, on the right hand size vertically if the shape color lines are horizontal or on the top horizontally if the shape color lines are vertical.

Make the grid size 10 by 10; place the shape in exactly the same spot with the same colors. If the strips of colors are going across, then make a solid vertical light blue line all the way to the right. If the strips of colors are going up and down, then make a solid horizontal light blue line all the way at the top.
The rule for this task is to extend a light blue line on the edge of the grid based on the direction of the lines in the original shape.

In this task i start by copying the image and looking if the pattern in the input was horizontal or vertical. If its vertical i draw a blue line along the top of the grid and if it is horizontal i draw a blue line along the right side of the grid.
Copy from input. If the lines are horizontal, draw a full vertical light blue line on the far right. If the lines are vertical, draw a full horizontal light blue line at the very top.

10x10 grid - Copy the shape exactly in the same position but add a light blue line on the far right side going all the way down from top to bottom(not sure if it matters but it is here because that's the way the lines on the shape are pointing)
keep the original design. If the lines are vertical, make the entire top line light blue. If the lines are horizontal, make the entire right side light blue.
Select correct number of squares. Draw two objects. Copy the multicolored object. Draw blue object across top of structure.
The answer requires a square object in the centre of the grid which must have the same grid area.

I copy the input and then look at the shape presented. If that shape is horizontal i will draw a blue line along the right hand side of the grid and if it vertical i draw a blue line along the top of the grid.

Recreate the first image exactly, keeping the grid size the same. Then, add a light blue line that extends completely across either the top row or the far right row. If the coloured lines in the image are horizontal, the light blue line is on the far right. If the lines are vertical, then the light blue line is at the top of the grid. Make sure it full length of the line and is one one square thick.

The rule for this one was to put a vertical cyan line on the right hand side of the grid if the coloured tiles were all horizontal, and if the coloured tiles were vertical you would be a horizontal cyan row along the top of the grid.

Using the same size grid as the input, a light blue line of squares is added to the full width or height of the grid, perpendicular to the orientation that the block consisting of several shorter lines of colour is facing

the grid and object are identical but with a pale blue section one cell wide on one edge.  if the cells are in blocks of colour going across the width then the pale blue section is on the far right.  if the blocks of colour are going down the grid then the pale blue section is along the top line of cells on the grid

the grid and objects are identical except for one pale blue addition.  if the main object has cells with colour going across the grid , all the cells on the last column on the right  hand side are pale blue.  if the coloured cells are columns of colour then there is a pale blue band one cell wide across the top of the grid.

the grid and the object are identical in size and position.  there is also a pale blue row or column .  if the main object is made from coloured rows then the furthest right column is coloured pale blue.  if the main  object is made up of coloured colums then the very top row is a pale blue row

Recreate the grid including the current shape and match the colors. If the colors run horizontally on the shape create a light blue border down the right most column. If the colors run horizontally create a light blue border across the top row. The border should only be one block wide.

Recreate the grid and shape exactly as is. If the colors on the shape run vertically make a row of light blue squares on the top row of the grid. If the colors run horizontally make a light blue column on the right most column of the grid.
add a blue line along the edge that the colours in the main block are pointing
create a blue line on the side where the main square colours are pointing at
add a blue line to the edge that the main square colours are pointing at

In the input grid, you will see a 'block' of some number of colored stripes. The stripes will be either horizontal, or vertical, and the number of stripes may vary. First, copy the size and shape of the input grid. next, copy the block over, exactly in the same position, size, and colors as it appears in the input grid. Next, you will add a light blue bar along one edge. If the stripes are horizontal, you will fill the entire rightmost column with light blue (a single column, one square wide, the entire vertical length of the grid). If the stripes in the block are vertical, you will fill the entire topmost row with light blue (one square high, the entire horizontal width of the grid).
You want to only include/keep the objects that have horizontal lines in them and omit any shapes that do not have horizontal lines in them while keeping the grid size the same.
Copy the input grid exactly, including the grid dimensions and the shapes. Now remove any shapes that have vertical coloured lines through them. Keep any shapes that have horizontal lines.
Copy from input and then select the colour black, use black to remove all the shapes that contain vertical lines.
Objects with horizontal stripes of different colors running through them need to be copied as is in the grid. Anything else is deleted.
Ensure that the black grid is of the same dimensions.  Delete every shape with vertical stripes in it's entirety.  Do nothing with anything with horizontal stripes.
Identify all objects within the grid that are composed of/contain vertical lines. Remove the aforementioned shapes by colouring them in black to match the background.
Copy from input, then make the entire grid black, by doing this you are removing all the shapes that have vertical lines. There will be no shapes left on the grid.
Copy from input. Remove all objects with vertical stripes.
Find any object with horizontal stripes and leave it as is, same colors and position. Delete any shape with vertical stripes. Leave the grid unchanged.
The grid is 12 height x 21 width. Copy from input keeping any shapes with horizontal lines going across them, ignore any shapes with vertical lines (delete them by covering with black).

Change your grid to the same size as the input. Copy across exactly any shapes that have horizontal coloured lines. Ignore shapes that have vertical lines. These do not need to be copied to your grid. The colours should match the shape you are copying.

Look at the input grid, there will be shapes with colours in horizontal and vertical lines. The answer is the same grid size as the input and you will need to only draw the shapes that have horizontal colours (lines that go left to right on the grid) in the same size, location and colours as the input.
Use the same grid size and colors from the input. Copy the input exactly as is. Using blue, draw a line across the entire side of the grid that the objects are closest too.
follow the instruction
just follow demonstration

Keep everything the same from the original grid. Use the exact same size and create all of the same objects with the same exact colors. If the objects have horizontal space/spaces between them, place a blue stripe in the left column. If the objects have vertical space/spaces between them, place a blue stripe in the top row.
Use the same grid size as in the input and select a minimum space side as a object.

Set the output to the same dimensions as the input. Draw all of the shapes exactly as you see in the input, maintaining all of the same colors. If there are an even number of objects, fill the entire top row with dark blue. If there are an odd number of objects, fill the entire left column with dark blue.

In this task I start by copying what is in the input. I looked at both axis and found which one had more space between our objects and the edges. On that axis i then found which side whether left, right, above or below was the closest to the objects. I then draw a blue line against this side.
Copy the original grid. Now, look for the side which the shapes are closest to. Fill that line of the grid in blue!
Copy grid from input. Draw a blue line in the edge column that the grouping of shapes is the closest to.

You want to copy the grid/block location (copy from input) the object and grid that you see. You then want to create a solid blue line on the horizontal corner or vertical corner from top to bottom or left to right on the axis or side that both objects occupy the most blocks. If they occupy more blocks on the left and are close to the middle you would create a vertical line out of blue boxes from top to bottom on the left. If they occupy more in the top quadrant, you would crate a line that's blue from the top left corner to the top right corner.

make the grid the same size as the example. add a one square thick dark blue line against the wall of the side of the grid closest to all of the shapes.  the blue stripe should be top to bottom or side to side  covering one entire side of the grid and all the shapes should be EQUALLY close to the line.

Copy the input grid exactly to the output.  There will multiple identical shapes the same row or column.  If they are in a horizontal row, identify if they are closer to the top or bottom and make a solid blue line on the side the shapes are closer to.  If they are in a vertical column, identify if they are closer to the left or right and make a solid blue line on the side the shapes are closer to.
Copy test input grid. Change the colour of every first cell on the left of the grid to blue, forming a vertical blue line on the far left of the grid.
Transform both shapes in the image. But replace the red lines with green lines for the second image below.
Make the second shape's red blocks green
Copy everything from input. If you have any horizontal red lines, turn them green.
Turn any horizontal red lines green.
Same grid size.  Recolor the red squares to green.
Leave the grid the same size and then migrate over all of the shapes that are in the grid. Then go to every horizontal line and turn it green instead of red.
Copy the grid exactly as it is. There will be shapes with red horizontal or vertical lines inside of them. Change the horizontal lines to green.

So copy the input grid over to the solution. You should have some sort of larger or shapes of a non-red color and inside each of those shapes should be lines either vertical or horizontal. Keep all the vertical lines red but any of the horizontal lines you'll want to change over and make them green. So it will look almost identical to the original just that the horizontal lines are no longer red but green.
copy the grid and arrangement from the input and then change all the red blocks arranged in a horrizontal line green
copy from input, the inner horizontal cells should be green, the vertical red
Use the same size grid as the input grid and copy the objects. Where objects are surrounded by another object, and their shape is horizontal, change their colour to green.

The shape in the test input grid should resemble a border on the outside with shapes on the inside. From the shapes on the inside, change the color of the horizontal facing shapes to green and change the color of the vertical facing shapes to red.
Just copy the input, same grid size and same shapes. The only thing you will change are the inner horizontal lines and change the color of those inner horizontal lines to green.
Recreate the shapes, but instead of red horizontal squares, make them green
Copy the input grid. Then change the color of any horizontal line of tiles to green.

resize the grids to be the same. copy the patterns to the new grid, same size and colors. Now look at the pattern of the interior, if the lines are vertical in the interior then keep them red, if the lines in the interior are horizontal then change them to green.
copy the input shape and grid over then turn all the blocks forming a horizontal line green
Copy task. Horizontal red bars are changed to green and horizontal green bars are changed to red.
The rule for this was was that any horizontal line of red tiles was to be transformed into green tiles. this colour change only applies to red tiles within a closed shape.
Make the whole line/column on the side of your grid green depend on the direction of your shape
Grid should be identical to one on left, if not resize grid to match. Copy object position and color. Place green line that runs along the complete right side of grid.

Copy the gird and object exactly. The add a horizontal or vertical line to the furthest edge of the grid that is directly above or to the right of the longest flat line of the object. the row added will be all green

Resize the grid as it is given. 

Recreate the object in the graph as given without changing its color or orientation. 

Determine the direction (vertical or horizontal) of the end lines (two lines) of the object. 

If the direction of the line ends of object are horizontal, place a green line horizontally at the very top of the graph so that is reaches the entire width. 

If the direction of the line ends of object are vertical, place a green line vertically at the very right side of the graph so that is reaches the entire height.
Copy the same pattern but insert a green line one cell deep and the full width at the top of the cell grid.

Along the side of the inner shape that has two full, clear rows between the shape and the edge colour the entire row or column against the side green.  The green line should not intersect with the yellow shape at all and should always be 1 full row away from it along it's length.
The solution is relatively simple. You need to copy the grid and shape exactly as per the example, with the only addition you need to make being a row of green cells along the top of the page.

Copy the grid exactly from the input. Identify the two parallel lines that should make up the edges of the shape. Using these, on the grid, create a green line that meets the following three conditions: 1. It should be parallel with the edge lines you just identified. 2. It should be on the edge of the grid on the side farthest from the shape. 3. It should go across the entire length/width of the grid.

Resize the grid to 10x10. Copy the yellow image exactly as the current picture. Once complete, fill in the last row on the right side going down with green - there should be 10 x green blocks filled on the furthest right.
copy over the grid and image exactly then change either the top most row or the rightmost row to green so that it runs parallel to the longest bar in the image
The examples showed the green line followed parralel to the yellow lines most nearest.

Copy the test grid size and image into the target grid. The yelllow pattern should be touching the left hand border of the grid, but leave the right border open. Fill every square on the right border in green.

Copy the input grid exactly to the output. If colored shape in the input is taller than it is wide, draw a green line from top to bottom along the right edge. If the colored shape in the input is wider than it is tall, draw a green line from side to side at the top of the output.

Change the grid to 14x14.  Copy the object exactly.  If it is more horizontal, then make a green line all the way across the top.  If  the object is vertical, make a green line all the way down the very right side.

Keep the grid size the same as the original. Keep all of the blocks and the colors exactly the same too. If the object looks more vertically aligned, place a solid green stripe for the whole far-right column. If the object looks more horizontally aligned, place a solid green strip across the whole top row.
Determine if height or the width is greater and if height is greater place a green line on the right hand side, else place a green line on the top
Keep the same sized grid and the same blue blocks exactly, and since it's an up/down design, add a row of green blocks to the far right like in demo #1

Create a grid exactly the same in height and width as the original.  Recreate the original shape in the same colour, size and position as the original.  Completely fill in the top single row in green if this does not touch the original shape and can leave a single blank row between this line and the shape.  If this cannot be done, completely fill in the far right hand column in green instead.

Use the same grid size as in the input. Copy the object from the input. If the object has a horizontal orientation, add a green line all the way across the top of the grid. If the object has a vertical orientation, add a green line all the way down the right side of the grid.
A green ceiling is added.
Color in a line of green grid along the right side for a shape that is vertical and along the top for one that is horizontal.

Here if the shape is up and down then you add a green border to the right of the image. If the shape is more long ways then you add a green border to the top. Copy the gird over then select the single border for the top in this example all the way across and fill in with the green color.

Copy the initial image. Identify the overall shape of the non-black squares. If the overall shape is more vertical than horizontal, then repaint (green) a vertical line at the right edge of new image. If the overall shape is horizontal then repaint the top horizontal line green

Use the same grid size.  Copy the object.  If the object is longer vertically make the last/right most column in the grid green. If the object is longer horizontally, make the topmost row in the grid green
Add a green line at the edge of the grid, 1 square wide. The edge chosen is the one that goes along the existing shape.

Copy the image (same grid size and colours). In the solution grid you will need to add either a one square thick green line to the far right side of the grid, or the top row of the grid. 

To know which, count the distance between the blue square furthest to the left and to the right. Then count the distance between the blue square closest to the top and to the bottom of the image. If the distance between the left-most and right-most square is greater than the distance between the highest and lowest square, then the green line goes across the top of the grid.

If the distance between between the highest and lowest blue square is greater than the distance between left-most and right-most blue square, then the green line will go on the right hand side of the grid. 

Remember the line is green, only one square thick and goes either completely from left to right, or top to bottom.

copy from input
draw a green line down the far right column

Copy the grid exactly from the input. Identify the two colored squares that do not match the color of the rest of the shape. Using these two dots, form an imaginary line in your head connecting them, and then, on the grid, color in a line at the grids edge that is both parallel with your imaginary line, and does not cover any of the original shape.

You will see a shape with two squares a different color than the rest. Copy the grid then, fill in one of the lines on the outside of the grid (left, top, bottom, or right) in the color of those two squares. The line should only be one square wide or high and the length of the border on that side. I honestly have no idea how you decide which side of the grid to color. The example has it on the left twice and the top once and I can't discern a reason. Good luck and godspeed.

Copy the grid exactly to the output.  There will be 2 blocks of a different color, either in a vertical column or a horizontal row.  If the dots are in a vertical column, then put an entire solid column of that same color all the way on the left using the same color.  If the dots are in a horizontal row, then put an entire solid row of that same color all the way on the top using the same color.

Copy the input grid exactly. There needs to be a solid gray line above the design because the pattern is to add a line that comes from the accent color. Select the top line fully across and then fill it with gray.

Copy the grip exactly as it is in the input. Look for the two red dots. If the are vertically aligned add a vertical red line on the left most column. if they are horizontally aligned add the red line to the top most row.

reproduce as is, grid, color, location. there will be 2 misscolored blocks. they will appear to run either up and down or left and right. If they are up down, create a bar on the left side wall 1 block thicksnes that runs top to bottom. if they run left to right, the bar goes on the top. The color of the bar matches the 2 misscolored blocks.
Since the two red squares are vertical, make a red vertical line on the left of the image.

Keep grid size ,object, and colors the same as the input. Use the same color as the two different color grid squares from the input and drag a vertical line on from the top left square of the grid to the bottom left square.
Use the same grid size as in the input. There will an object with two red squares. Place one row of red squares along the object's side that has no red squares
create an orange line on the left hand margin of the grid

Change grid to 10x10.  Copy the exact object and colors.  If the two orange blocks are vertical, make a vertical orange line on the very left side.  If the two orange blocks are horizontal, make an orange line all the way across the top.

Keep the grid the same as the original. It should have all the same blocks and the same size. Here is the only change to make. Look at the two red squares. If the red blocks are vertical, place a solid red line that covers the whole left column. If the red blocks are horizontal, place a solid red line that covers the whole top row.
The image needs copied from the original one, exactly the same. A red column needs added to the very far left of the image.
Draw a red line parallel to the random blocks along the length of the grid. Make sure it also touches the main shape
Image will a central colored base with different colored appendages. They will all be different colors. Copy the image over exactly and delete the top and bottom appendages.

There is a complex object of many colors in the grid. It has multiple objects, each of whih is a different color, stuck together. Recreate the grid exactly, and turn the topmost and bottom-most colored objects black, leaving all of the others as is. It will look like the same object, but with its top and bottom bits cut off.
Remove and grid pieces that are above and below the main shape of the object.  Keep those that are to the left and right of it
Use the same grid size as in the input.  There will be three objects in the input in your answer.  Copy from input and remove the top and bottom objects.  Keep the colors the same.
Copy the grid size and objects from the input grid. If the main object body is green, remove coloured blocks that are above and below the green object.
use exact same grid and put all the shapes into same spot, but then erase the odd colored shapes into black at the top and bottom.
copy from input and remove the upper and lower part from the object.
Same grid. Remove the top and bottom squares.

Copy the test input grid and keep it that size. Think of the design as a center design with four patterns coming off of it. The top and bottom patterns need to be removed. Use the select feature to select the top square and then color it black. Then do the same with the bottom square. The center and left and right designs stay the same.
copy the grid and shape over from the input and then delete the alternate coloured shapes from the top and bottom of the green primary shape

Use the same grid size as the input. You should see one green shape surrounded by four different coloured shapes on the top, bottom, left, and right. You should copy everything you see exactly, however you should remove the two different coloured shapes at the top and bottom, so you are left with the green shape, and two different coloured shapes on the left and right.
Remove any shape that is a different colour to the centre shape that is located on the top and bottom of the centre shape
keep grid and colors the same. Remove color shapes on the top and bottom of main body . Duplicate what is left after that. it should look the same but with the bottom and lower colors missing
Find the green shape and remove the colored areas from the top and bottom.

First, copy from input. The grid size needs to be kept the same. Look at the shape. It has different parts made of different colours. The uppermost and lowermost parts with different colours respectively need to be omitted, so cover these parts with black, leaving the rest of the shape the same,

Use the same size grid as the input.There will be only one object in the input. In your answer, remove only the external color block(s) above and below the object. Keep all colors the same as the input
Keep the same grid size. Copy the target image exactly, using the same colours. However, omit the lower and upper vertical parts of the original shape.
Add more black background squares so that they cover up the isolated color blocks to the top and bottom of the image.

use the same size grid as the input. draw out the same green squares in the input, and the non-green squares on the right and left sides of the shape with the same colours as the input shape. leave out the top and bottom non-green squares.

Copy the input grid and objects. treat each colored set of squares in the input as an independent object in the output. Now color the vertical column objects yellow and the horizontal line objects orange.
Copy from input, grid should be 6 x 6. Replace any coloured lines that are vertical with yellow, and any horizontal with orange
Replace horizontal shapes with orange blocks and vertical shapes with yellow blocks. Same grid size.
Use the same size grid (6x6). Copy the shapes where they're placed. The only difference is that the horizontal pieces should be colored orange and the vertical pieces should be colored yellow.
Keep the grid size the same as well as the shapes and sizes of the blocks. The vertical lines should be changed to a yellow colour and the horizontal lines should be changed to an orange colour.
keep the pattern the same but change the colours to orange and yellow, the base is orange and the lines are yellow

Make the grid size 6 by 6. Then place all four vertical and horizontal lines in the same spot. The two lines that are colored the same, either exactly the same or different shades, should be colored orange. And then the other two lines should be colored yellow.
Keep the grid and position of the shapes the same, change the colors to make all vertical shapes yellow and all horizontal shapes orange

Recreate the grid as depicted. Assuming that each color represents a different object, recolor each object according to its vertical or horizontal orientation; Use yellow for vertical objects and orange for horizontal objects.
Change the vertical lines to yellow and the horizontal lines to orange
Use the same size grid as original, keep the shapes in the same size and position but all vertical lines become yellow and all horizontal shapes become orange
grid size the same, and objects are an exact replica, only all of the vertical cells colour yellow and all the horizontal cells colour orange
Copy the input as it is. Make the horizontal lines orange, perpendicular ones yellow, regardless of what color they were before.
use grid from the imput then change the horizontal lines to orange and the vertical lines to yellow

Choose 'Copy from input' button. The grid consists of horizontal shapes and vertical shapes. Choose 'Select' and for each vertical line (up and down) draw a box that contains the entire line, then click Yellow. Then, for each horizontal line (left and right) draw a box that contains the entire line, then click Orange.
put exactly same with standing grids are yellow and horizontal grids are orange

Match the Test input grid size. Select the horizontal colored objects and make them orange. Then select the vertical colored objects and make them yellow. If two objects are touching, be careful to not cross over into the other when changing their colors.
Resize the grid to 6 by 6, and recolor in the vertical blocks to yellow and horizontal blocks to orange.
Copy the size and location of the grid's shapes exactly, making the vertical shapes yellow and the horizontal shapes orange. The grid should remain the same size.
There will be several long lines composed of colored squares. Keeping the grid the same, change all vertical lines of squares to yellow, and all horizontal lines of squares to orange.
Use the same grid size. Copy the exact shapes. No matter the color, change the color of the vertical lines to yellow and the horizontal lines to orange.
